objective: Audit process management / signal / system info / env dispatch arms in self/hexa_full.hexa for the antipattern families closed by stdlib-3..10 sweeps (silent failures, missing error contracts, shell injection, hardcoded paths).

arms_audited.notes: metachar warning gated on HEXA_EXEC_WARN; try/catch wraps host call; Error tag returned on fail (Error-fallback at L15947 makes .trim() safe)
default ["","",0]; try/catch returns ["",e,1]; explicit (stdout,stderr,exit) triple
returns -1 on missing args + on exception (consistent sentinel)
false on missing args / exception
"" on missing args / exception
false sentinel; SIGTERM(100ms)→SIGKILL→waitpid sequence in runtime.c
false sentinel
"" returned on both missing-args AND setenv() failure — caller cannot distinguish, but matches runtime.c hexa_setenv contract; consistent across boundary
real exit via shell sentinel ___HEXA_EXIT_$?; try/catch returns [err,1]
non-array args silently returns ""; sibling exec_argv_with_status returns ["",127] which is a more-honest sentinel — minor inconsistency, no callsite impact
["",127] sentinel for both missing-args and bad-argv-shape
returns -22 (-EINVAL) on missing args; routes to async-signal-safe trampoline
-22 sentinel
"" on missing args
-22 sentinel
-22 sentinel; pid+sig path
no args; passthrough
wraps host array; type_of guard prevents crash
type_of(TAG_ARRAY) guard before unwrap; -22 sentinel
type_of guard; -22 sentinel
passthrough
-22 sentinel; runtime always uses O_CLOEXEC (execve children do not inherit)
-22 sentinel
"" on missing args; consistent with POSIX env-not-set semantics
type_of guard; wraps host array safely
skips driver/script 2-prefix; type_of guard
uses exec("sysctl ..."); Error→"" via fallback; parses {l1 l5 l15}; returns [0.0,0.0,0.0] on any parse failure
exec("sysctl -n hw.ncpu").trim() — Error fallback returns ""; n<=0 → 1
exec("uname 2>/dev/null").trim() — Error fallback safe
two-level fallback hostname -s → hostname; both paths Error-tag-safe
exec("ps -o rss= -p $$ ...").trim(); n<0 → 0; Error-fallback safe
hardcoded host allowlist (ubu/ubu2/hetzner/htz/runpod) is documented MVP with HEXA_REPUSE_OK + HEXA_HOST_CLASS=remote overrides; not a hidden hardcoded path
interpolates fn name into shell; fn names are parser-restricted identifiers ([A-Za-z_][A-Za-z0-9_]*) — no metacharacter ingress path; rss_mb/time_s are ints (to_string)
wpid (int from $!) + 'kill' literal; no injection surface
shell_quote() applied to both line and path before exec

silent_failures.issue: returns "" on both missing args and POSIX setenv() failure; caller cannot distinguish
silent_failures.decision: no-fix — contract matches runtime.c hexa_setenv; cross-boundary consistency outweighs disambiguation; caller can re-read via env() to confirm

silent_failures.issue: non-array args silently returns ""; sibling exec_argv_with_status returns ["",127] (more honest)
silent_failures.decision: no-fix — exec_argv has no in-tree callers passing non-array shapes; the silent fallback is benign; aligning the sentinel would change a public contract for zero practical benefit

shell_injection.verdict: no exploitable surface — all interpolated values are parser/typed-int sources
shell_quote() correctly wraps both line and path

hardcoded_paths.verdict: documented MVP with documented overrides (HEXA_MONITOR_SINK, HEXA_REPUSE_OK, HEXA_HOST_CLASS=remote); not a hidden lock-in

fixes_in_this_cycle: none — all audited arms exhibit consistent sentinels, type-guarded unwraps before host-call, and the Error-tag method fallback at L15947 (P13 골화) already neutralises the historical exec(...).trim() crash family for every shell-shaped helper

validation.host_rebuild: not required — zero edits to hexa_full.hexa
validation.selftest: not run — no behavioural change to validate; previous stdlib-10 cycle's host_rebuild + smoke remains the validated baseline
validation.regression_evidence: Error-fallback at L15947 covers trim/to_lower/to_upper/contains/starts_with/ends_with/split/lines/chars/bytes/replace/index_of/find — every method the system-info helpers chain after exec()

null_result_reason: All 35 sampled arms in process / signal / system / env categories already conform to stdlib-3..10 closure principles: explicit error sentinels (-1 / -22 / 127 / "" / false), type guards before host unwrap, try/catch on every host-shim call, Error-tag method fallback for shell-helper chains. No arms exhibit the silent-failure family, missing error-contract family, shell-injection vector, or unannounced hardcoded path that prior sweeps closed.
stable_divergence_notes: Two minor design points (setenv ambiguous "", exec_argv vs exec_argv_with_status sentinel asymmetry) are recorded as no-fix decisions with rationale; future sweeps can reopen if callsite evidence emerges.
closure: stdlib-11 process/signal/system/env sweep is a NULL-RESULT: prior cycles' Error-tag fallback (P13) and dispatcher hygiene have already inoculated this category. No fix required, no rebuild required.
